summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: Evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature: –1 to 13°C at coil suction outlet

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Dehumidifying Coil Sensible Heat Ratio
summary: Typical range for the air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils.
evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0

title: Valve Authority for HVAC Coils
summary: Recommended valve authority range for HVAC coil applications to maintain proper flow characteristic.
evidence_quote: Typically, authorities in the range of 30 to 50% (for piping systems with 2:1 branch-to-riser pressure drop ratios) are adequate for HVAC coil applications

title: Low-Temperature Heating System Design
summary: Typical design parameters for low-temperature heating systems.
evidence_quote: They are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.

title: Return Air Plenum Temperature Difference
summary: Typical temperature difference in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck during cooling season.
evidence_quote: The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof deck is usually higher by 2 to 3 K during the air-conditioning season than in a ducted return.

title: Filter-Drier Requirement for POE/PVE Lubricants
summary: Requirement for using filter-driers with hygroscopic lubricants to prevent moisture contamination.
evidence_quote: Filter-driers are required by virtually all manufacturers when using hygroscopic lubricants such as polyol ester (POE) and polyvinyl ether (PVE), particularly for split-system units, to remove any moisture introduced during installation or servicing.

title: Noise Source Pinpointing
summary: Procedure to determine if noise originates from the outlet or the system.
evidence_quote: The cause of a noise complaint can usually be pinpointed as outlet or system sounds by removing the outlet’s core during operation. If the noise remains essentially unchanged, the system is the source. If the noise is significantly reduced, the outlet is likely the source.

title: Coil Air Face Velocity for Dehumidification
summary: Typical air face velocity range for selecting dehumidifying coils in comfort applications.
evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 2.0 to 2.5 m/s air face velocity.

title: Coil Airflow Resistance Comparison
summary: Comparison of airflow resistance between a dry sensible cooling coil and a dehumidifying coil.
evidence_quote: A completely dry coil, removing only sensible heat, offers approximately one-third less resistance to airflow than a dehumidifying coil removing both sensible and latent heat.

title: Hydrophilic Fin Coating Benefit
summary: Reported reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil compared to a new untreated coil.
evidence_quote: Tests have shown as much as a 30% reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil as opposed a new untreated coil.
